Title: Natural variation in CHELATASE SUBUNIT I-A increases grain weight and enhances wheat yield

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes photosynthesis, biomass production, and ultimately grain weight/yield
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AAA+ ATPase magnesium chelatase ChlI/CHLI subunit
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the ATPase I subunit of magnesium chelatase that drives insertion of Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX during the first committed step of chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required to form the first committed intermediate leading to chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control/first key step in chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases chlorophyll content, photosynthetic rate, flag leaf area, grain size, thousand-grain weight, grain weight per spike, and field grain yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHLI-A was identified as the causal gene underlying the major yield QTL QGwps.pku-7A. 2. Presence or increased expression of CHLI-A raised chlorophyll a/b content and net photosynthetic rate. 3. Native-promoter complementation in Shi4185 and UBI-driven overexpression in Fielder increased grain size, thousand-grain weight, grain weight per spike, grain weight per plant, and field yield. 4. CRISPR loss-of-function mutants reduced chlorophyll, photosynthesis, grain weight, and yield. 5. Promoter haplotype Hap-E showed higher CHLI-A expression and heavier grains, indicating breeding value.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; presence-absence variation analysis; RNA-seq differential expression; transgenic complementation; overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; chlorophyll measurement; photosynthetic rate measurement; field yield trials; haplotype association analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in young spikes, immature grain, and seedling leaves; Hap-E promoter associated with higher expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in young spikes, immature grain, seedling leaves, and flag leaves; upregulated in transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field trials at low and high sowing densities; greenhouse long-day growth for RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProCHLI-A (native promoter, genomic rescue); UBI promoter (constitutive overexpression)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Reduced-representation resequencing/SNP analysis; RNA-seq; germplasm resequencing; raw sequence accession CRA017446
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No evident trade-off on grain number per spike, spike number per unit area, or most spike traits; chli-A loss reduced plant height and leaf growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value breeding target for improving wheat yield by increasing grain weight without major yield-component trade-offs; useful strategies include restoring absent CHLI-A, increasing copy number or expression, and selecting the high-expression Hap-E promoter haplotype
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CHLI-A shows presence-absence variation in a subset of elite wheat cultivars; gene absent in Shi4185 and some North China cultivars; further overexpression remains beneficial even in CHLI-A-containing backgrounds

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Component of magnesium chelatase complex involved in Mg2+ insertion into protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Under positive selection during domestication from wild einkorn to domesticated einkorn
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. XP-CLR analysis detected a strong selection signature at CHLD-A during einkorn domestication. 2. The paper suggests CHLD genes may also contribute to grain-related traits, but no direct functional validation was performed here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Population resequencing and XP-CLR selection scan
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Domestication comparison: wild einkorn vs domesticated einkorn
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Global resequencing panel of diploid, tetraploid, and hexaploid Triticum accessions; XP-CLR analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential candidate for future study in grain-related trait improvement, but application not established here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as selected during domestication, but not experimentally demonstrated as causal for metabolite or yield changes in this study
